arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer objdump 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -E 'binutils|llvm' 2>/dev/null | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try fetching debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server for the BuildId
# BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"

# Try the ELF debuginfo format
echo "=== Trying debuginfo via symbol server ==="
curl -sS -f -L -o /tmp/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 || echo "debuginfo fetch failed"

# Also try fetching the library itself (sometimes has symbols)
echo "=== Trying library via symbol server ==="
curl -sS -f -L -o /tmp/libmonosgen-2.0.so \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 || echo "library fetch failed"
arguments.description: Fetch debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Fetch debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this tombstone. The key frames are from `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`.

To symbolicate these frames, I would need the debug symbols for that specific build of `libmonosgen-2.0.so`. The BuildId is `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`.

There are several approaches:
1. Use a symbol server (like Microsoft's symbol server) to download the debug symbols


2. Use the `dotnet-symbol` tool to fetch symbols
3. Check if there's a debuginfod server available

Microsoft publishes .NET runtime symbols on their symbol server at `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols`, and for ELF binaries on Linux/Android, I can construct the URL using the BuildId to fetch the specific debug symbols. Alternatively, debuginfod servers can also be queried for the symbols.

Now I'm trying to fetch the symbols using the BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. The Microsoft symbol server uses a specific URL format for ELF files, and I can also try using tools like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` if the debug info is available locally. Let me attempt a few different approaches to retrieve the symbols. a .NET global tool, though the SDK might not be available. Let me try a more direct approach: check what symbolicating tools are on hand, fetch the debug symbols from Microsoft's server, and work through the addresses. I've got several offsets from the libmonosgen-2.0.so library that need to be resolved, all tied to the same BuildId.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer objdump 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -E 'binutils|llvm' 2>/dev/null | head -5

toolArgs.command:
# Try fetching debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server for the BuildId
# BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"

# Try the ELF debuginfo format
echo "=== Trying debuginfo via symbol server ==="
curl -sS -f -L -o /tmp/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 || echo "debuginfo fetch failed"

# Also try fetching the library itself (sometimes has symbols)
echo "=== Trying library via symbol server ==="
curl -sS -f -L -o /tmp/libmonosgen-2.0.so \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 || echo "library fetch failed"
toolArgs.description: Fetch debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
